title: 11 Proven Herbal Creams for Over Bleeding During Periods and Menstrual Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce uterine cramps and heavy bleeding by regulating hormone balance and improving blood vessel function.

Kaempferol is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce blood loss and alleviate menstrual cramps by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and shed its lining.

Tannins help to reduce bleeding during periods by acting as a natural astringent that constricts blood vessels and reduces the flow of blood, thereby giving the body time to heal and stop excessive bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, is a type of herb that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. Its creams are particularly beneficial for women who experience heavy or irregular periods, leading to over bleeding. The main active compound in red clover creams is isoflavone, which has a natural hormone-balancing effect. This helps to regulate the menstrual cycle, reducing the severity of heavy bleeding. Additionally, red clover creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the lining of the uterus, reducing bleeding and discomfort. Some studies have also shown that isoflavones can help to strengthen blood vessels, further reducing the risk of over bleeding. By using Trifolium pratense creams, women can experience relief from heavy periods and over bleeding, without the need for harsh medications or invasive treatments. The creams can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams for period relief, you will need dried Angelica root, a carrier oil, and a beeswax or coconut oil base. Start by making a tea by steeping a tablespoon of dried Angelica root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool and strain the liquid. Next, mix the cooled tea with a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil in a 1:2 ratio, and let it sit for 2-3 weeks to allow the Angelica's properties to infuse into the oil. After the infusion, strain the mixture and discard the solids. Now, melt a mixture of beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, then slowly pour the infused oil into the melted wax, and mix well until you get a smooth and creamy texture. This herbal cream can help to ease period cramps and reduce bleeding, but it is essential to note that it should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment, and you should consult with a healthcare professional before using it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus cream is used to help with heavy bleeding during periods. It is made from the herb Vitex agnus-castus, also known as chasteberry, which has been used for centuries to treat various women's health issues. The cream works by helping to regulate the menstrual cycle and balance hormone levels. This is because it contains compounds that act on the brain, telling it to produce less prostaglandins - hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to heavy bleeding. By reducing the production of these substances, the cream can help to reduce the severity of heavy bleeding and make periods less painful. It also helps to regulate the menstrual cycle, reducing the frequency and duration of periods. This can be especially helpful for women who experience irregular periods, or those who experience prolonged or heavy bleeding. Additionally, the cream may also help to reduce symptoms of PMS, such as mood swings and breast tenderness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with over bleeding during periods, you will need some dried Angelica root, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a few other simple ingredients. First, fill a clean glass jar with a handful of dried Angelica root, and then pour enough carrier oil over it to cover the root completely. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days to help the oil extract the herb's properties. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, in a small saucepan, gently heat a mixture of beeswax, shea butter, and a few drops of vitamin E oil until the beeswax melts, then slowly pour the infused Angelica oil into the mixture, stirring constantly to create a smooth, creamy texture. Let the mixture cool and solidify, then transfer it to a clean, sterilized container and store it in the fridge to prevent spoilage. This homemade Angelica cream can be applied topically to the lower abdomen or inner thighs to help soothe and regulate menstrual bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa cream is made from the root of the black cohosh plant, which has been used for centuries to help with menstrual problems. This natural remedy is believed to reduce heavy bleeding during periods by helping to relax the uterus and ease cramps. The cream is thought to block the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and bleed heavily. By blocking these prostaglandins, the cream may help to reduce the severity of menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding. The cream is applied directly to the skin, usually on the lower abdomen, and is said to be absorbed quickly into the body, where it can start working to ease symptoms. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream to help with heavy bleeding during periods, start by gathering fresh or dried Angelica root, which is rich in compounds that help reduce bleeding. First, dry the Angelica root by spreading it out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth, and letting it air dry for 2-3 weeks. Once dry, grind the Angelica root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the ground Angelica root with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool slightly before transferring it to a clean glass jar. Let the cream cool and solidify completely before using it to apply topically to the lower abdomen, where it can help reduce heavy bleeding and ease menstrual cramps.

intro_desc: If you're experiencing heavy or prolonged bleeding during your periods, using herbal creams can be a helpful solution. One herb that may help is Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover. It contains isoflavones, which can help to reduce bleeding by thinning the blood and improving circulation to the uterus. This can lead to lighter and more regular periods. Another herb that can be beneficial is Vitex agnus-castus, also known as chaste tree. It can help to regulate hormone imbalances that may be contributing to heavy bleeding, and also has a natural anti-inflammatory effect that can help to reduce pain and discomfort.  Heavy bleeding during periods can be more than just an inconvenience - it can be a sign of an underlying health issue that needs to be addressed. If left unchecked, heavy bleeding can lead to anemia, fatigue, and other complications. By addressing the issue, you can improve your overall health and quality of life. For example, you may be able to reduce the frequency of your periods, or alleviate symptoms such as cramping and bloating. You may also find that you have more energy and are able to participate in activities that you enjoy. By taking care of your reproductive health, you can take back control of your body and live a healthier, happier life.  Herbal creams that contain Trifolium pratense and Vitex agnus-castus can be applied directly to the skin to help reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms. These creams can be especially helpful for women who are experiencing heavy bleeding due to hormonal imbalances or other underlying health issues. By using these creams, you can help to regulate your hormones and improve your overall health, leading to a better quality of life.
supplementary_best: For over bleeding during periods, a combination of herbal creams from 'Trifolium pratense' (red clover), 'Calendula officinalis' (calendula), and 'Hamamelis virginiana' (witch hazel) can be effective.  Red clover contains isoflavones, which help in regulating menstrual cycles and reducing heavy bleeding. Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease cramps. Its antiseptic properties can also help prevent infection from heavy bleeding.  Witch hazel is a natural astringent that can help stop the bleeding by tightening the blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the uterus. Its anti-oxidant properties can also help protect the body from damage caused by heavy bleeding.  This combination is effective because it targets the issue from multiple angles. Red clover addresses the hormonal imbalance, calendula reduces inflammation and prevents infection, and witch hazel stops the bleeding. Using these creams together can provide relief from over bleeding during periods and promote overall uterine health.
supplementary_avoid: If you experience heavy bleeding during your periods, you should avoid using herbal creams that contain the following herbs: Fenugreek (Trigonella foenum-graecum) - as it can stimulate estrogen levels, which may worsen bleeding. Saw palmetto (Serenoa repens) - as it may interfere with hormone regulation and worsen heavy bleeding. Sage (Salvia officinalis) - as it can decrease estrogen levels, which may lead to lighter bleeding, but if you're already experiencing heavy bleeding, sage may make it worse. Wild yam (Dioscorea villosa) - as it can stimulate estrogen levels, which may worsen bleeding. Evening primrose oil (Oenothera biennis) - as it may cause the uterus to contract, which can increase bleeding. Red clover (Melilotus officinalis) - as it contains isoflavones, which can stimulate estrogen levels and worsen bleeding. Blue cohosh (Caulophyllum thalictroides) - as it can stimulate the uterus and increase bleeding.
supplementary_other_remedies: Cramping and heavy bleeding during periods can be managed with some natural remedies beyond herbal creams. Cold compresses can help ease cramping by numbing the pain and reducing inflammation in the uterus. This is because cold temperatures can slow down the release of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to cramping. Applying a cold compress to the lower abdomen for 15-20 minutes at a time can be very effective. Warm baths or showers can also help relax the muscles and reduce cramping by releasing endorphins, the body's natural painkillers. Some people find that drinking ginger tea or taking ginger supplements helps to ease cramping and heavy bleeding due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids, such as salmon and walnuts, can also help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Additionally, practicing relaxation techniques like deep breathing, meditation, or yoga can help manage stress and reduce menstrual cramping.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience heavy bleeding during their periods may also be prone to other health issues. For example, they may have low iron levels, which can cause fatigue, weakness, and pale skin. This is because iron is essential for carrying oxygen in the blood, and a lack of it can lead to anemia. Medicinal herbs like dandelion root and nettle leaf can help increase iron levels. Dandelion root stimulates digestion, allowing the body to absorb iron better, while nettle leaf is rich in iron and can be consumed as a tea or added to food.  Heavy bleeding can also lead to an imbalance of hormones, particularly estrogen and progesterone. This can cause mood swings, anxiety, and depression. Herbs like chasteberry and passionflower can help regulate hormones and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. Chasteberry contains flavonoids that can help balance estrogen levels, while passionflower is a natural sedative that can calm the nervous system.  Another issue associated with heavy bleeding is a weakened immune system. This can make people more susceptible to infections and diseases. Herbs like echinacea and yarrow can help boost the immune system. Echinacea stimulates the production of white blood cells, which fight off infections, while yarrow has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi.  Additionally, heavy bleeding can lead to a condition called endometriosis, where tissue from the uterus grows outside the uterus and can cause pain, inflammation, and scarring. Herbs like turmeric and ginger can help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory properties, while ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce pain.  Finally, heavy bleeding can lead to a condition called fibroids, which are non-cancerous growths in the uterus that can cause pain, bleeding, and infertility. Herbs like red clover and black cohosh can help reduce the size of fibroids and alleviate symptoms. Red clover contains isoflavones that can help balance hormones and reduce inflammation, while black cohosh can help reduce the size of fibroids and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
